summary: PR 2604 links the Kirby Yoshi-special translation unit by changing `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` from NonMatching to Matching, narrowing its split range, and moving the remaining 0x8010A930-0x8010D740 code/data into a new nonmatching `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` plus header. It also updates Kirby includes/prototypes, motion-state wiring, and a few item/fighter references affected by the new capture-yoshi header ownership.

key_files.role: Narrowed `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` text/sdata2 ranges and introduced a new `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` split for the remaining range.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: splits.txt hunk changes ftkirbyspecialyoshi text 0x80109260-0x8010A930, sdata2 0x804D9558-0x804D9560, and adds ftkirbycaptureyoshi text 0x8010A930-0x8010D740, sdata2 0x804D9560-0x804D95B0

key_files.role: Changed `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` to Matching and added `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` as NonMatching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py hunk replaces Object(NonMatching, ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c) with Object(Matching, ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c) and Object(NonMatching, ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c)

key_files.role: New nonmatching source file holding the moved capture-yoshi and related Kirby-copy special code, including `fn_8010A930` and callbacks/static helpers formerly covered by the larger yoshi-special split.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: new file mode 100644 with `#include "ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h"`, `fn_8010A930`, `ftCo_CaptureKirbyYoshi_*`, `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`, and static declarations such as `fn_8010B2E8`, `fn_8010C344`, `fn_8010C44C`, `fn_8010CFB0`

key_files.role: New header for declarations exposed from the capture-yoshi split.
Large deletion consistent with moving most remaining nonmatching code out while preserving the now-linked yoshi-special range.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: added 4 lines, deleted 1472 lines, 7 hunks

key_files.role: Removed static prototypes for functions now local to the new capture-yoshi source.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: removes static declarations for `fn_8010B2E8`, `fn_8010C344`, `fn_8010C44C`, and `fn_8010CFB0`

key_files.role: Replaced a direct local declaration of `fn_8010A930` with an include of the new capture-yoshi header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds `#include "ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h"` and removes `void fn_8010A930(Fighter_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*);`

key_files.role: Header cleanup affected by moving declarations to the new capture-yoshi header.
Small motion-state table/reference adjustment for the new header or split.
Small item-side include/reference adjustment affected by the Kirby split.
Small item-side include/reference adjustment affected by the Kirby split.

what_changed: The build configuration now treats `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` as Matching instead of NonMatching.
The original `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` split was shortened to text `0x80109260-0x8010A930` and sdata2 `0x804D9558-0x804D9560`.
A new `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` split was added for text `0x8010A930-0x8010D740` and sdata2 `0x804D9560-0x804D95B0`, and configure.py adds it as NonMatching.
A new `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` source file and `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` header were added.
`ftkirby.c` now includes `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` instead of declaring `fn_8010A930` locally.
Several static declarations were removed from `ftKb_Init.static.h`, with matching declarations visible in the new capture-yoshi source excerpt.

The PR also touched `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c`, `ftmotionstates.c`, `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c`, and `itpeachtoad.c`, but the loaded excerpt only provides line-count metadata for most of these small dependent edits.

smart_moves.text: Isolated the still-nonmatching trailing capture-yoshi range into its own translation unit so the earlier `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` range could be marked Matching.
Moved `fn_8010A930` out of a source-local declaration in `ftkirby.c` into an owned header include, aligning call sites with a real declaration surface.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftkirby.c adds `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` and removes local prototype

smart_moves.text: Kept unresolved code explicitly NonMatching after the split rather than implying the entire old yoshi-special range was linked.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a translation unit contains a contiguous matched prefix and an unresolved tail, a split update can preserve matching progress by narrowing the matched TU range and moving the remaining range into a new NonMatching TU.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py marks old file Matching and new file NonMatching

decomp_lessons.lesson: After moving functions across a split boundary, update the owning header/include relationship instead of leaving source-local prototypes at call sites.

naming_conventions.text: The new file and header use the subsystem-specific stem `ftkirbycaptureyoshi`, matching the moved capture-yoshi functions such as `ftCo_CaptureKirbyYoshi_*` and `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`.
Unidentified local/static functions retain address-style names such as `fn_8010B2E8`, `fn_8010C344`, and `fn_8010CFB0` rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Locals in the excerpt use common role names such as `fp`, `attacker_fp`, `gobj`, `attacker_gobj`, `jobj`, and `victim` where roles are clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: TU/split-boundary adjustment: shorten the matching file at address `0x8010A930` and move the rest into a new file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is supported as a PR tactic by the split/configure evidence, but the loaded evidence does not include objdiff/checkdiff logs.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an owned header for cross-file declarations after the split.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftkirby.c` includes `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` and removes direct `fn_8010A930` prototype
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Consistent with truthful-header practice, but the exact header contents are not shown in the excerpt.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The new nonmatching file contains narrow codegen steering constructs including `PAD_STACK` and `#pragma dont_inline` around some functions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim` has `#pragma dont_inline on`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and reset
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Because this file remains NonMatching and no verifier logs are loaded, these should not be promoted as accepted style; they are evidence of temporary matching tactics in an unresolved file.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use a split boundary to link a matched prefix while keeping the unresolved tail NonMatching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2604 shortened `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` to end at `0x8010A930`, added `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` for `0x8010A930-0x8010D740`, and changed configure.py so the old file became Matching while the new file remained NonMatching.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Move declarations to the new owning header after a source split
accepted_candidate_records.text: `ftkirby.c` stopped declaring `fn_8010A930` locally and instead included `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h`, making the new split own the declaration surface.
